response.value: I'll help you look at your config files. Let me read the main configuration files in your setup.

I can see your Arch Linux configuration! Here's what you have set up:

## **Hyprland** (Wayland Compositor)
- Monitor: 1464x919@59.97Hz
- **Gaps:** 5px inner, 10px outer
- **Border:** 4px with gradient blue theme (Catppuccin-inspired)
- **Apps:** Kitty terminal, Nautilus file manager, Rofi launcher, Firefox browser
- **Autostart:** Waybar, hyprpaper, mako, network manager, blueman, clipboard history

## **Kitty** (Terminal)
- **Opacity:** 0.85 with dynamic transparency
- **Font:** monospace, 11.0pt
- **Theme:** Catppuccin Mocha colors
- **Tabs:** Bottom powerline style

## **Waybar** (Status Bar)
- **Position:** Top, 18px height
- **Layout:** Workspaces/Window | Clock | Media/Status
- **Shows:** Workspace indicators, window titles, clock with calendar, MPRIS media info
- There's a typo: `"position": "tp"` should be `"top"`

## **Theme Colors**
- **Background:** Dark blue-black (#0f0f17)
- **Accent:** Blue gradient (Catppuccin blue tones)
- **Palette:** Nordic/Catppuccin mix

Would you like me to fix the waybar position typo, or help you customize any specific aspect of your config?

edits.text: # Fresh Install Script for Arch Hyprland Setup
# This script installs all necessary packages for the configuration
# Check if running on Arch-based system
echo "Error: This script requires an Arch-based system with pacman."
# Check if yay is installed, install if not
echo "1. Log out and select Hyprland from your display manager"
echo "2. Or run 'Hyprland' from a TTY"
echo "Your config files are already in ~/.config/"

toolCalls.name: create_file
toolCalls.arguments:
{"filePath": "/home/alex/.config/install.sh", "content": "#!/bin/bash
# Fresh Install Script for Arch Hyprland Setup
# This script installs all necessary packages for the configuration

set -e  # Exit on error

echo "=================================="
echo "Arch Hyprland Config Installer"
echo "=================================="
echo ""

# Check if running on Arch-based system
if ! command -v pacman &> /dev/null; then
    echo "Error: This script requires an Arch-based system with pacman."
    exit 1
fi

# Check if yay is installed, install if not
if ! command -v yay &> /dev/null; then
    echo "Installing yay AUR helper..."
    sudo pacman -S --needed git base-devel
    git clone https://aur.archlinux.org/yay.git /tmp/yay
    cd /tmp/yay
    makepkg -si --noconfirm
    cd -
    rm -rf /tmp/yay
fi

echo ""
echo "Installing core Wayland/Hyprland packages..."
yay -S --needed --noconfirm \
    hyprland \
    hyprpaper \
    waybar \
    rofi \
    mako \
    polkit-gnome

echo ""
echo "Installing terminal and applications..."
yay -S --needed --noconfirm \
    kitty \
    firefox \
    nautilus

echo ""
echo "Installing system utilities..."
yay -S --needed --noconfirm \
    network-manager-applet \
    blueman \
    pavucontrol \
    cliphist \
    wl-clipboard \
    grim \
    slurp \
    playerctl

echo ""
echo "Installing fonts..."
yay -S --needed --noconfirm \
    ttf-font-awesome \
    ttf-jetbrains-mono-nerd \
    noto-fonts \
    noto-fonts-emoji

echo ""
echo "=================================="
echo "Installation Complete!"
echo "=================================="
echo ""
echo "Next steps:"
echo "1. Log out and select Hyprland from your display manager"
echo "2. Or run 'Hyprland' from a TTY"
echo ""
echo "Your config files are already in ~/.config/"
echo ""
"}

toolCallRounds.response:
Created `install.sh` in your `.config` directory! 

The script will:
- Check for and install `yay` if missing
- Install all core Hyprland/Wayland packages
- Install your configured applications (kitty, firefox, nautilus, rofi)
- Install system utilities (network, bluetooth, audio, clipboard, screenshots)
- Install recommended fonts (Nerd Fonts, Font Awesome, Noto)

To run it: `./install.sh` (it will prompt for sudo password when needed)
